text:
NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT
Comprehensive Solid Tumor Genomic Profiling

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Patient Name: Margaret Chen
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Sex: Female
Medical Record Number: [REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: [REDACTED], MD — Thoracic Oncology
Specimen ID: SP-2024-ADRNL-0291
Specimen Source: CT-guided core needle biopsy, right adrenal gland
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue core
Date of Procedure: [REDACTED]
Date Received in Laboratory: [REDACTED]
Date of Report: [REDACTED]
Report Status: FINAL
Clinical Indication: Acquired resistance profiling — metastatic non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma), EGFR L858R-mutant, progressive disease following osimertinib (Tagrisso) therapy
Primary Diagnosis (provided): NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 21 L858R-mutant, stage IV (adrenal metastasis at progression)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SPECIMEN ADEQUACY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Tumor Cellularity (Estimated): 55%
DNA Yield (Input): 78 ng
Library Quality: PASS
Mean Coverage Depth: 847x (tumor); 312x (matched normal, peripheral blood)
Percent Bases at ≥100x Coverage: 98.7%
Overall Specimen Assessment: ADEQUATE FOR COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING

Note: Pathologic review of H&E slide confirms metastatic adenocarcinoma consistent with lung primary origin (TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A focally positive, CK7 positive). Specimen suitable for all requested analyses.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ASSAY METHODOLOGY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Platform: Hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing (Illumina NovaSeq 6000)
Panel: Comprehensive solid tumor panel — 648 cancer-related genes (full coding sequence and selected intronic regions)
Analysis: Somatic single nucleotide variants (SNVs), small insertions/deletions (indels), copy number alterations (CNAs), structural rearrangements/gene fusions, tumor mutational burden (TMB), microsatellite instability (MSI)
Germline analysis performed with matched peripheral blood normal sample; somatic variants confirmed by subtraction of germline polymorphisms.
Minimum reportable allele frequency: 1% (for SNVs/indels); copy number gains ≥6 copies and losses to ≤1 copy reported
Fusion detection: RNA-based fusion analysis performed (RNA extracted from same FFPE block)
Bioinformatics Pipeline Version: SomaticPipeline v4.7.2
Variant Classification: ClinGen/CGC/VICC framework (Oncogenic, Likely Oncogenic, Variant of Uncertain Significance, Likely Benign, Benign)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GENOMIC FINDINGS — SUMMARY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT ALTERATIONS DETECTED:

  1. EGFR p.Leu858Arg (L858R) — Exon 21 point mutation
     Classification: ONCOGENIC (Tier I — Strong clinical significance)
     Variant Type: Activating missense mutation
     Allele Frequency (tumor): 44%
     [PRESENT IN PRIOR SPECIMEN — see comparison below]

  2. EGFR p.Thr790Met (T790M) — Exon 20 point mutation
     Classification: ONCOGENIC (Tier I — Strong clinical significance; acquired resistance)
     Variant Type: Resistance missense mutation
     Allele Frequency (tumor): 31%
     [NEWLY DETECTED — not present in baseline specimen dated [REDACTED]]

  3. TP53 p.Arg175His (R175H) — Exon 5 missense mutation
     Classification: ONCOGENIC (Tier I)
     Variant Type: Loss-of-function/dominant negative missense mutation; hotspot
     Allele Frequency (tumor): 47%
     [PRESENT IN PRIOR SPECIMEN]

  4. PIK3CA p.Glu545Lys (E545K) — Exon 9 (helical domain) missense mutation
     Classification: ONCOGENIC (Tier I — Strong clinical significance; acquired resistance)
     Variant Type: Activating missense mutation; PI3K pathway activator
     Allele Frequency (tumor): 19%
     [NEWLY DETECTED — not present in baseline specimen dated [REDACTED]]

  5. CDKN2A — Homozygous deletion (intragenic; whole gene loss)
     Classification: ONCOGENIC (Tier I)
     Variant Type: Bi-allelic tumor suppressor loss; affects p16INK4a and p14ARF isoforms
     Estimated Copy Number: 0 (homozygous loss)
     [PRESENT IN PRIOR SPECIMEN]

  6. NKX2-1 (TTF-1) — Focal amplification, chromosome 14q13.3
     Classification: LIKELY ONCOGENIC (Tier II)
     Variant Type: Transcription factor amplification; lineage survival oncogene in lung adenocarcinoma
     Estimated Copy Number: 12 copies (high-level amplification, relative to ploidy)
     [PRESENT IN PRIOR SPECIMEN]

NO CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT ALTERATIONS DETECTED IN THE FOLLOWING GENES OF INTEREST:
  - KRAS: Wild-type (no mutation detected)
  - NRAS: Wild-type
  - BRAF: Wild-type (no V600 or non-V600 alteration)
  - MET: No exon 14 skipping mutation; no amplification detected (copy number 2.1, within normal range)
  - ERBB2 (HER2): Wild-type; no amplification (copy number 2.0)
  - RET: No rearrangement or fusion detected (RNA-based)
  - ROS1: No rearrangement or fusion detected (RNA-based)
  - ALK: No rearrangement or fusion detected (RNA-based)
  - FGFR1/2/3/4: Wild-type; no amplification
  - STK11 (LKB1): Wild-type
  - KEAP1: Wild-type
  - SMAD4: Wild-type
  - RB1: Wild-type (no loss)
  - PTEN: Wild-type (no loss, no mutation)
  - EGFR exon 20 p.Cys797Ser (C797S): NOT DETECTED (allele frequency <1%; below limit of detection)
  - EGFR exon 18-21 insertions (non-classical): NOT DETECTED
  - EGFR exon 20 insertions: NOT DETECTED

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB) AND MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5 mutations/Mb
  Interpretation: LOW (cutoff for TMB-High: ≥10 mutations/Mb per FDA-approved companion diagnostic)
  Note: TMB values in EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinomas are characteristically low and this finding is concordant with the established molecular profile. Low TMB is consistent with a diminished likelihood of benefit from immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy in this context.

Microsatellite Instability (MSI) Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
  Method: Analysis of 92 microsatellite loci
  Fraction of Unstable Sites: 0.8% (threshold for MSI-High: >20%)
  Interpretation: No evidence of mismatch repair deficiency by MSI analysis.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COMPARATIVE GENOMIC ANALYSIS: BASELINE vs. PROGRESSION SPECIMEN
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Baseline Specimen: CT-guided left upper lobe biopsy (Specimen ID: SP-[REDACTED], obtained at initial diagnosis, prior to any systemic therapy)
Current Specimen: CT-guided right adrenal metastasis biopsy (Specimen ID: SP-2024-ADRNL-0291, obtained at radiographic progression following approximately 14 months of osimertinib therapy)

Alterations PRESENT AT BOTH TIME POINTS (clonally maintained):
  - EGFR p.L858R: 38% AF (baseline) → 44% AF (progression) [slight enrichment]
  - TP53 p.R175H: 42% AF (baseline) → 47% AF (progression) [stable/slight enrichment]
  - CDKN2A homozygous deletion: Present (baseline) → Present (progression)
  - NKX2-1 amplification: Present (baseline) → Present (progression)

Alterations NEWLY DETECTED at progression (consistent with acquired under therapeutic selection pressure):
  - EGFR p.T790M: NOT DETECTED at baseline → 31% AF at progression
    Interpretation: Classic on-target acquired resistance mechanism to third-generation EGFR TKI (osimertinib/Tagrisso). T790M restores EGFR kinase domain steric hindrance, preventing effective drug binding. This mutation is paradoxically the primary resistance mechanism to first-generation TKIs (erlotinib, gefitinib) and can emerge as a secondary resistance mechanism during osimertinib therapy through a distinct clonal evolution pathway. Its detection at high allele frequency (31%) indicates substantial clonal burden and likely reflects a dominant resistance clone.
  - PIK3CA p.E545K: NOT DETECTED at baseline → 19% AF at progression
    Interpretation: Activating mutation in the helical domain of the PIK3CA catalytic subunit (p110-alpha). E545K is a well-characterized oncogenic hotspot that disrupts an inhibitory interaction between the helical and regulatory (p85) subunits of PI3K, resulting in constitutive PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activation. This mutation is detected at a lower allele frequency than the primary EGFR driver (19% vs. 44%), consistent with a subclonal or late-acquired resistance mechanism. PIK3CA pathway activation provides bypass signaling that permits tumor cell survival despite EGFR inhibition and may contribute to the compound resistance phenotype observed in this patient.

Note: No EGFR C797S (exon 20) mutation was detected. C797S, which confers resistance to osimertinib by disrupting the covalent bond between osimertinib and the EGFR cysteine residue, is not present above the limit of detection. The absence of C797S in the context of T790M retention is clinically notable, as C797S in cis with T790M would confer triple-mutant resistance (L858R/T790M/C797S) with very limited therapeutic options. The absence of C797S preserves certain therapeutic strategies.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS — COMPLETE SUMMARY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Gene             Chromosome    CNA Type              Copies    Significance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CDKN2A           9p21.3        Homozygous deletion   0         Oncogenic
NKX2-1 (TTF-1)   14q13.3       Focal amplification   12        Likely Oncogenic
EGFR             7p11.2        Polysomy (low-level)   4         Uncertain
MYC              8q24.21       Low-level gain         3         Uncertain/not reportable

No high-level amplification of MET (7q31), ERBB2 (17q12), FGFR1 (8p11.23), or other clinically actionable genes was detected.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GENE FUSIONS / STRUCTURAL REARRANGEMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

RNA-based fusion analysis performed. No clinically relevant gene fusions detected.

Specifically, the following fusions were assessed and not detected:
  - ALK (EML4-ALK and all known ALK fusion partners): NOT DETECTED
  - ROS1 (CD74-ROS1 and all known ROS1 fusion partners): NOT DETECTED
  - RET (KIF5B-RET and all known RET fusion partners): NOT DETECTED
  - NTRK1/2/3 fusions: NOT DETECTED
  - NRG1 fusions: NOT DETECTED
  - MET exon 14 skipping event: NOT DETECTED

Note: Absence of ALK, ROS1, RET fusions is concordant with prior IHC/FISH negativity at baseline and consistent with the established EGFR-driven oncogenesis in this tumor.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ADDITIONAL SOMATIC VARIANTS (Variants of Uncertain Significance / Low-tier)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Gene       Nucleotide Change     Protein Change     AF     Classification
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ATM        c.2251A>G             p.Ile751Val        6%     VUS (Tier III)
AXIN1      c.1538C>T             p.Ser513Leu        4%     VUS (Tier III)
CREBBP     c.3764G>A             p.Arg1255His       3%     VUS (Tier III)

Note: The above variants are classified as Variants of Uncertain Significance (VUS) based on available evidence. They are not considered clinically actionable at this time. ATM p.I751V has no established germline or somatic significance in this context; germline testing correlation is advised at the discretion of the treating team if family history warrants. No ATM loss-of-function frameshift or nonsense variant was identified.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
INTERPRETATION AND GENOMIC CONTEXT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This comprehensive genomic profiling of the right adrenal metastasis was performed in the setting of radiographic progressive disease following approximately 14 months of osimertinib (Tagrisso) therapy for stage IIIB/IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma.

The dominant molecular findings are as follows:

1. ON-TARGET EGFR RESISTANCE — EGFR T790M ACQUISITION:
The detection of EGFR p.T790M at a variant allele frequency of 31% represents a high-burden, clonally dominant on-target resistance mechanism to osimertinib. While osimertinib was designed to overcome T790M resistance to first-generation EGFR TKIs, T790M can paradoxically re-emerge during osimertinib therapy through mechanisms that are not completely elucidated but likely involve clonal evolution from a minor pre-existing T790M-containing subclone or de novo acquisition. The T790M substitution replaces the threonine gatekeeper residue with methionine in the EGFR kinase domain, creating steric clash with the osimertinib binding pocket while preserving kinase activity. At 31% allele frequency — approximately 70% of the L858R AF — T790M appears to be present in a substantial proportion of tumor cells, consistent with a primary resistance clone driving progression.

Importantly, no EGFR C797S mutation was detected. C797S, which abolishes osimertinib covalent binding and would preclude use of third-generation TKIs even in combination strategies, is absent. This is relevant because T790M without C797S may retain partial sensitivity to certain fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors currently in clinical development.

2. BYPASS SIGNALING — PIK3CA E545K ACQUISITION:
The newly acquired PIK3CA p.E545K helical domain activating mutation (19% VAF) defines a second, subclonal resistance mechanism. E545K creates constitutive, RAS-independent PI3K activation by disrupting an inhibitory charge interaction between the p110-alpha catalytic subunit and the p85 regulatory subunit. This results in persistent AKT/mTOR signaling independent of EGFR inhibition status, enabling cell survival and proliferation even when EGFR is effectively suppressed. The subclonal nature of this variant (19% vs. 44% for the primary EGFR driver) suggests it may represent a secondary resistance event that accelerated progression or contributed to a more aggressive metastatic phenotype. Concurrent T790M and PIK3CA mutations define a compound resistance profile that may require combinatorial therapeutic strategies targeting both mechanisms.

3. CLONALLY MAINTAINED CO-DRIVERS:
The TP53 p.R175H hotspot mutation (47% VAF), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, and NKX2-1/TTF-1 amplification were present at baseline and remain present at this progression specimen, confirming their clonal maintenance throughout disease evolution and treatment exposure. TP53 R175H is a dominant-negative hotspot mutation that abolishes wild-type p53 function and can additionally sequester and inactivate residual wild-type p53, amplifying oncogenic effects. Co-existing TP53 mutation with EGFR-activating mutations is a recognized feature associated with shorter PFS on EGFR TKI therapy in several retrospective datasets. CDKN2A bi-allelic loss eliminates both p16INK4a (CDK4/6 pathway de-repressor) and p14ARF (MDM2 antagonist/p53 stabilizer), further compromising the p53/cell cycle regulatory axis. NKX2-1 amplification is a lineage survival amplification characteristic of lung adenocarcinoma and does not confer TKI resistance per se but may contribute to transcriptional dependencies.

4. ABSENCE OF MET AMPLIFICATION:
MET amplification is one of the most common bypass resistance mechanisms in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, occurring in approximately 15-25% of cases at progression. The absence of MET amplification (copy number 2.1, within diploid range) in this specimen argues against MET-bypass as the operative resistance mechanism and narrows the primary resistance drivers to the on-target T790M mutation and the PIK3CA E545K bypass.

5. ABSENCE OF HISTOLOGIC TRANSFORMATION MARKERS:
No RB1 loss, no neuroendocrine markers (based on gene expression inference), and preservation of TTF-1 amplification are consistent with maintained adenocarcinoma identity without evidence of small cell transformation — a recognized uncommon but clinically important resistance mechanism requiring careful correlation with pathology.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
THERAPEUTIC IMPLICATIONS (INFORMATIONAL — CLINICAL CORRELATION REQUIRED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Alterations with Potential Therapeutic Relevance (to be interpreted in clinical context):

  EGFR L858R / T790M (co-occurring, T790M without C797S):
  - Standard platinum-based doublet chemotherapy (e.g., carboplatin + pemetrexed) is an established option for post-EGFR TKI progression in NSCLC.
  - Fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors targeting EGFR with T790M and/or compound resistance mutations (e.g., BLU-945, BBT-176, and related investigational agents) may have activity, particularly in the absence of C797S.
  - Amivantamab (RYBREVANT; EGFR/MET bispecific antibody) combined with platinum-based chemotherapy has demonstrated activity in post-third-generation EGFR TKI progression settings.
  - Osimertinib re-challenge at reduced dose or in combination strategies is under investigation.

  PIK3CA E545K:
  - Alpelisib (Piqray; PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor) has demonstrated activity against PIK3CA-mutant tumors in other histologies and is in clinical development for NSCLC.
  - Combined EGFR inhibitor + PI3K inhibitor strategies are being explored in clinical trials for patients with co-existing EGFR and PIK3CA alterations (PIK3CA inhibition as combination partner to address bypass resistance).
  - Clinical trial enrollment should be strongly considered given the compound resistance profile (EGFR T790M + PIK3CA E545K) that may be specifically addressable by novel combinations.

  TMB-Low / MSS:
  - Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy is not expected to provide substantial benefit based on the low TMB, MSS status, and EGFR-mutant context (multiple datasets demonstrate poor outcomes with ICI monotherapy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC).

IMPORTANT NOTICE: Therapeutic implications listed above are provided for informational purposes only to aid clinical decision-making. Final treatment decisions must be made by the treating physician in the context of full clinical information, patient preference, performance status, organ function, and available clinical trials. This report does not constitute a treatment recommendation.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QUALITY CONTROL METRICS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Metric                              Value          Pass/Fail
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tumor Content (estimated H&E)       55%            PASS
DNA Input (ng)                      78             PASS (threshold: ≥20 ng)
Uniformity of Coverage              98.7% >100x    PASS
Mean Coverage (tumor)               847x           PASS
Mean Coverage (normal)              312x           PASS
Off-target Rate                     3.1%           PASS
Duplicate Read Rate                 12.4%          PASS
Library Complexity                  Adequate       PASS
Contamination Rate (GATK)           0.3%           PASS (<2.0%)
RNA QC (fusion analysis)            RIN equiv 5.2  PASS (threshold: ≥4.0 for FFPE)
All quality metrics met. Results are reportable.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
LIMITATIONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. Spatial heterogeneity: This analysis represents genomic profiling from a single biopsy specimen of a single metastatic site (right adrenal gland). Intratumoral and interlesional genomic heterogeneity may result in other tumor sites (e.g., brain metastases, primary lung mass) harboring different resistance mechanisms not captured in this specimen.

2. Sensitivity limits: Variants present at allele frequencies below 1% may not be reliably detected. A low-level C797S (below limit of detection) could theoretically be present and may emerge with further selective pressure.

3. Epigenetic and transcriptional resistance mechanisms: This panel does not assess epigenetic modifications, promoter methylation, or transcriptional reprogramming (e.g., epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition, lineage plasticity) that may contribute to therapeutic resistance.

4. FFPE artifact: Low-frequency variants detected at <5% allele frequency were subjected to additional quality filters to mitigate FFPE-related C>T artifact; ATM and other low-VAF variants listed as VUS were verified as non-artifactual by orthogonal review.

5. Dynamic clonal evolution: The genomic landscape may continue to evolve under ongoing therapeutic selection pressure. Repeat profiling at subsequent progression is recommended.
